title: A retractable idler roller frame
abstract: This invention relates to a retractable idler roller frame 10 that includes a base 12, which is operatively mounted to a support structure 11 of a conveyor, and an idler carrier 13 which is releasably connected to the base 12 and vertically displaceable. A relative to the base 12 between an operative or lifted position, where idler rollers 15 are operatively in abutment with a conveyor belt 14 above; and a retracted or lowered position, where the idler carrier 13 has been dropped to a lower position relative to the base 12 and where the idler rollers 15 are operatively spaced away from the belt 14 to permit access to the rollers 15 for removal and replacement of same.
claims: Claims 1 . A retractable idler roller frame for a conveyor, the idler roller frame including: a base; and - an idler carrier to which a plurality of idler rollers are removably mounted, the idler carrier being releasably connected to the base and displaceable relative to the base between an operative position in which the idler rollers are operatively in abutment with a conveyor belt, and a retracted position in which at least one idler roller is operatively spaced away from the belt to permit access to one or more of the idler rollers operatively mounted to the idler carrier for removal and replacement of same, wherein the idler roller frame is configured such that displacement of the idler carrier relative to the base between its operative and retracted positions is achieved with a lever by way of a first order lever action. 2. A retractable idler roller frame as claimed in claim 1 , wherein, during displacement of the idler carrier between its retracted position and its operative position, the base serves as a fulcrum for the lever. 3. A retractable idler roller frame as claimed in claim 1 or 2, wherein, during displacement of the idler carrier from its retracted position to its operative position, effort is applied to one end of the lever the magnitude of which exceeds a load applied by a displacement means of the idler carrier to an opposite end of the lever. 4. A retractable idler roller frame as claimed in claim 1 or 2, wherein, during lowering of the idler carrier from its operative position to its retracted position, effort is applied to one end of the lever the magnitude of which is less than a load applied by a displacement means of the idler carrier to an opposite end of the lever. 5. A retractable idler roller frame as claimed in claim 1 , wherein the idler carrier is also pivotably connected to the base whereby it is pivotably displaceable relative to the base. 6. A retractable idler roller frame as claimed in claim 1 , wherein the point at which the idler carrier is connected to the base is vertically displaceable relative to the base as a result of the first order lever action, thereby facilitating displacement of the idler carrier between its respective positions. 7. A retractable idler roller frame as claimed in claim 3 or 4, wherein the base includes a pair of spaced apart pillars, and wherein the idler carrier includes a pair of mounting brackets, each of which is configured to engage one pillar of the base whereby the idler carrier is supported between the pillars. 8. A retractable idler roller frame as claimed in claim 7, wherein the mounting bracket is slidably displaceable relative to its corresponding pillar to permit displacement of the idler carrier between its respective positions as a result of the first order lever action. 9. A retractable idler roller frame as claimed in claim 8, wherein the displacement means is connected to the mounting bracket of the idler carrier. 10. A retractable idler roller frame as claimed in claim 9, wherein the displacement means defines at least one height-adjusting orifice for receiving the lever. 1 1 . A retractable idler roller frame as claimed in claim 9, wherein the displacement means defines a pair of staggered height-adjusting orifices, each orifice configured to operatively receive a separate lever. 12. A retractable idler roller frame as claimed in claim 10 or 1 1 , wherein each pillar defines a slot for slidably accommodating its corresponding mounting bracket and at least one opening for allowing passage of the lever therethrough during displacement of the idler carrier. 13. A retractable idler roller frame as claimed in claim 12, wherein each pillar defines a pair of openings for allowing passage of a pair of levers therethrough during displacement of the idler carrier. 14. A retractable idler roller frame as claimed in claim 1 3, which is configured for sequential alignment of each opening of the pair of openings with one of a pair of staggered height-adjusting orifices defined by the displacement means, as the idler carrier is displaced from one of its positions to the other. 15. A retractable idler roller frame as claimed in any one of claims 8 to 14, wherein each pillar includes connecting means which is configured releasably to connect to the mounting bracket of the idler carrier in order to secure it in its operative position. 16. A retractable idler roller frame as claimed in claim 1 5, wherein the connecting means includes a plurality of discrete connection points to allow height adjustment of the idler carrier with respect to the base whereby the idler carrier is operatively supportable at different height positions on the base. 17. A retractable idler roller frame as claimed in any one of the preceding claims, wherein displacement of the idler carrier relative to the base between its respective positions is brought about as a result of dual first order lever action effected between the base and a lifting formation of the idler carrier. 18. A method of accessing one or more idler rollers operatively mounted to a retractable idler roller frame as claimed in any one of the preceding claims, the method including subjecting the idler carrier to first order lever action using the lever in order to displace the idler carrier relative to the base from one of its positions to the other. 19. A method as claimed in claim 1 8, which includes subjecting opposing ends of the idler carrier to first order lever action using a lever in order to displace the idler carrier relative to the base. 20. A method as claimed in claim 18 or 20, which includes: engaging, using a first lever, a first part of a displacement means of the idler carrier; subjecting the displacement means to first order lever action using the first lever; engaging, using a second lever, a second part of the displacement means of the idler carrier; and subjecting the displacement means to further first order lever action using the second lever.
